name: Vadouvan-Spiced Slow-Roasted Leg of Lamb
description: A semi-boneless leg of lamb coated in a vadouvan compound butter with orange zest, rosemary, and pink peppercorns, slow-roasted for a showpiece halal French cooking result. No alcohol, no pork.
keywords: halal french cooking, vadouvan lamb, slow roasted leg of lamb, halal dinner party
recipeIngredient: 1 semi-boneless leg of lamb, shank bone in, 3.8-4kg (8½-9 lb)

recipeInstructions.text: Make the vadouvan butter by combining softened butter with vadouvan, orange zest, rosemary, kosher salt, crushed pink peppercorns, and black pepper until uniform.
Remove lamb from refrigerator 1 hour before roasting and pat surface dry with paper towels.
Place lamb in a large roasting pan and coat the entire surface with the compound butter.
Roast uncovered at 230°C (450°F) for 25 minutes to set the crust.
Lower oven to 150°C (300°F), pour 240ml water into the bottom of the roasting pan, and return to oven.
Roast at 150°C (300°F) for 2 hours 45 minutes to 3 hours, basting every 45 minutes, until internal temperature reaches 60°C (140°F) for pink or 68°C (155°F) for medium.
Rest the lamb loosely tented with foil for 20-30 minutes before carving.
Skim fat from pan juices, taste for salt, and serve alongside the carved meat.
